Shrine|en|Kamayama Shrine|fr|Sanctuaire Kamayama|ja|竈山神社|simple|Kamayama Shrine|WD=Q11597743|1=Kamayama Shrine}} {{commons category|Category:Shizuhi-jinja}} [[JA:静火神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28693031}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Shizuhi Shrine |Image = [[File:Shizuhi-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and shrine buildings |Location = Wada, {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}} |Coordinates= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|12|10.64|N|135|12|28.86|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shizuhi Shrine}} |Deity = Shizuhi Ōkami |Shintai = |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Grand Shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
An extramural subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kamayama Shrine|Q11597743|WD=Q11597743|1=Kamayama Shrine}} |Founded = Unknown
(before {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 11 ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}})) |Main Hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage site = Ki Sansho-sha |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Shizuhi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{Coordinate list}} [[File:Shizuhi-jinja kyuuchi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Former site monument of Shizuhi Shrine (foreground) and Mount Yakushi, where the current shrine stands]] '''Shizuhi Shrine''' (しずひじんじゃ / しづひじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Wada, {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Today, it is an extramural subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kamayama Shrine|Q11597743|WD=Q11597743|1=Kamayama Shrine}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Shima Shrine|Q17212913|WD=Q17212913|1=Shima Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}, Wakayama City), it is known as the “Ki Sansho-sha” (Three Shrines of Ki) {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following single kami {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}: :* Shizuhi Ōkami According to documents of the Ugai clan, the shrine’s hereditary priests, the deity was identified as {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Musubi no Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. Another theory, found in the *Zoku Fudoki*, holds that the “Ki Sansho-sha” were branch enshrinements of the three deities of Isonokami—Itsu no Take no Mikoto, Ōyatsuhime no Mikoto, and Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto—and identifies the deity of Shizuhi Shrine as Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. Yet another view considers Shizuhi to have been a “fire-quelling deity” of Kii Province, known as the “Land of Trees” {{Sfn|Kamayama Shrine and Shizuhi Shrine (Kami-gami)|1986|pp=316–318}}. In addition, the *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}* states that Funatama Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}, was the clan deity of the Ki clan and the main shrine of “Shima Shrine, Shizuhi Shrine, and Date Shrine.” === History === === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. Originally, the shrine stood about 200 meters east of its current location ({{Coord|34|12|9.46|N|135|12|37.07|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Former site of Shizuhi Shrine}}) {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. According to the *Zoku Fudoki*, Shizuhi Shrine had fallen into ruin before the {{ill|lt=Einin|Q1327307|WD=Q1327307|1=Einin}} era ({{ill|lt=1293|Q5557|WD=Q5557|1=1293}}–{{ill|lt=1299|Q5564|WD=Q5564|1=1299}}), but a document of the Hinokuma Shrine from {{ill|lt=Chōkyō|Q968843|WD=Q968843|1=Chōkyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1487|Q6652|WD=Q6652|1=1487}}) records that one *to* of ritual rice was allocated to Shizuhi Shrine {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. According to the *Nanki Tokugawa-shi*, by around {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1723|Q6921|WD=Q6921|1=1723}}) the shrine had again fallen into ruin; in that same year, Wakayama Domain rebuilt the shrine buildings on Mount Yakushi (Maeyama / Amagiri-yama; the present site), and Kamayama Shrine assumed its administration {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. === Overview === In the national histories, together with Date Shrine and Shima Shrine (Shima Jinja), Shizuhi Shrine is recorded as having received promotions in divine rank: from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 11 ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}); to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}); to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}); and to Junior Third Rank in Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}) {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}*, it is listed under {{ill|lt=Kii Province|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} as “Shizuhi Shrine, Myōjin Grand Shrine,” placing it among the {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}; the *{{ill|lt=Kii no Kuni Jinmyōchō|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}* records it as “Senior First Rank, Shizuhi Ōkami” {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. In all cases, it is ranked higher than Kamayama Shrine. It is said that together with {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Shima Shrine|Q17212913|WD=Q17212913|1=Shima Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}, Wakayama City), it was known as the “Ki Sansho-sha” {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}. References to the Ki Sansho-sha appear beginning with a rice storage ledger from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549|1=Eishō (Heian period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1048|Q19359|WD=Q19359|1=1048}}), as well as in the *{{ill|lt=Chūyūki|Q11363231|WD=Q11363231|1=Chūyūki}}* entry for {{ill|lt=Tennen|Q1193599|WD=Q1193599|1=Tennin (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1109|Q19515|WD=Q19515|1=1109}}) and in the four-verse sacred songs of the *{{ill|lt=Ryōjin Hishō|Q3010584|WD=Q3010584|1=Ryōjin Hishō}}* {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}. According to the *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}*, the origin of the Ki Sansho-sha lies in the three ships used by {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} for the {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}, which she had {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} enshrine {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}. Furthermore, since Wada Village was formerly a shrine estate of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma Shrine and Kunikakasu Shrine|Q888184|WD=Q888184|1=Hinokuma Shrine}}, the deity of Shizuhi was closely connected with those shrines and with the {{ill|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11604953|WD=Q11604953|lt=hereditary governors of Kii Province|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}. At Kusa-no-miya, enshrined within the grounds of those shrines, it is said that on September 15 the deity of Shizuhi made a sacred procession and the Shizuhi Festival was held {{Sfn|Kamayama Shrine and Shizuhi Shrine (Kami-gami)|1986|pp=316–318}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; however, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}) it was merged into Inari Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of Kamayama Shrine {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}{{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 25 ({{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|WD=Q18597|1=1950}}), shrine buildings were constructed on the original Mount Yakushi site and the deity was transferred there {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. === Divine ranks === * Records of divine rank promotions in the Six National Histories ** {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 11 ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}) 11th month, 3rd day: from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}*) – recorded as “Shizuhi no Kami.” ** {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}) 10th month, 21st day: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}*) – recorded as “Shizuhi no Kami.” ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) 1st month, 27th day: from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – recorded as “Shizuhi no Kami.” ** Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}) 10th month, 17th day: from Senior Fourth Rank, == Commons category (P373) == * Shizuhi-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '和歌山県和歌山市和田', 'language': 'ja'} == parent organization or unit (P749) == * {{ill|Kamayama Shrine|lt=Kamayama Shrine|en|Kamayama Shrine|fr|Sanctuaire Kamayama|ja|竈山神社|simple|Kamayama Shrine|WD=Q11597743|1=Kamayama Shrine}} {{commons category|Category:Shizuhi-jinja}} [[JA:静火神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28693031}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Shizuhi Shrine |Image = [[File:Shizuhi-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and shrine buildings |Location = Wada, {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}} |Coordinates= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|12|10.64|N|135|12|28.86|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shizuhi Shrine}} |Deity = Shizuhi Ōkami |Shintai = |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Grand Shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
An extramural subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kamayama Shrine|Q11597743|WD=Q11597743|1=Kamayama Shrine}} |Founded = Unknown
(before {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 11 ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}})) |Main Hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage site = Ki Sansho-sha |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Shizuhi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{Coordinate list}} [[File:Shizuhi-jinja kyuuchi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Former site monument of Shizuhi Shrine (foreground) and Mount Yakushi, where the current shrine stands]] '''Shizuhi Shrine''' (しずひじんじゃ / しづひじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Wada, {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Today, it is an extramural subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kamayama Shrine|Q11597743|WD=Q11597743|1=Kamayama Shrine}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Shima Shrine|Q17212913|WD=Q17212913|1=Shima Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}, Wakayama City), it is known as the “Ki Sansho-sha” (Three Shrines of Ki) {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following single kami {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}: :* Shizuhi Ōkami According to documents of the Ugai clan, the shrine’s hereditary priests, the deity was identified as {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Musubi no Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. Another theory, found in the *Zoku Fudoki*, holds that the “Ki Sansho-sha” were branch enshrinements of the three deities of Isonokami—Itsu no Take no Mikoto, Ōyatsuhime no Mikoto, and Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto—and identifies the deity of Shizuhi Shrine as Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. Yet another view considers Shizuhi to have been a “fire-quelling deity” of Kii Province, known as the “Land of Trees” {{Sfn|Kamayama Shrine and Shizuhi Shrine (Kami-gami)|1986|pp=316–318}}. In addition, the *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}* states that Funatama Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}, was the clan deity of the Ki clan and the main shrine of “Shima Shrine, Shizuhi Shrine, and Date Shrine.” === History === === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. Originally, the shrine stood about 200 meters east of its current location ({{Coord|34|12|9.46|N|135|12|37.07|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Former site of Shizuhi Shrine}}) {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. According to the *Zoku Fudoki*, Shizuhi Shrine had fallen into ruin before the {{ill|lt=Einin|Q1327307|WD=Q1327307|1=Einin}} era ({{ill|lt=1293|Q5557|WD=Q5557|1=1293}}–{{ill|lt=1299|Q5564|WD=Q5564|1=1299}}), but a document of the Hinokuma Shrine from {{ill|lt=Chōkyō|Q968843|WD=Q968843|1=Chōkyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1487|Q6652|WD=Q6652|1=1487}}) records that one *to* of ritual rice was allocated to Shizuhi Shrine {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. According to the *Nanki Tokugawa-shi*, by around {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1723|Q6921|WD=Q6921|1=1723}}) the shrine had again fallen into ruin; in that same year, Wakayama Domain rebuilt the shrine buildings on Mount Yakushi (Maeyama / Amagiri-yama; the present site), and Kamayama Shrine assumed its administration {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. === Overview === In the national histories, together with Date Shrine and Shima Shrine (Shima Jinja), Shizuhi Shrine is recorded as having received promotions in divine rank: from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 11 ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}); to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}); to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}); and to Junior Third Rank in Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}) {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}*, it is listed under {{ill|lt=Kii Province|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} as “Shizuhi Shrine, Myōjin Grand Shrine,” placing it among the {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}; the *{{ill|lt=Kii no Kuni Jinmyōchō|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}* records it as “Senior First Rank, Shizuhi Ōkami” {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. In all cases, it is ranked higher than Kamayama Shrine. It is said that together with {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Shima Shrine|Q17212913|WD=Q17212913|1=Shima Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}, Wakayama City), it was known as the “Ki Sansho-sha” {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}. References to the Ki Sansho-sha appear beginning with a rice storage ledger from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549|1=Eishō (Heian period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1048|Q19359|WD=Q19359|1=1048}}), as well as in the *{{ill|lt=Chūyūki|Q11363231|WD=Q11363231|1=Chūyūki}}* entry for {{ill|lt=Tennen|Q1193599|WD=Q1193599|1=Tennin (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1109|Q19515|WD=Q19515|1=1109}}) and in the four-verse sacred songs of the *{{ill|lt=Ryōjin Hishō|Q3010584|WD=Q3010584|1=Ryōjin Hishō}}* {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}. According to the *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}*, the origin of the Ki Sansho-sha lies in the three ships used by {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} for the {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}, which she had {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} enshrine {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}. Furthermore, since Wada Village was formerly a shrine estate of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma Shrine and Kunikakasu Shrine|Q888184|WD=Q888184|1=Hinokuma Shrine}}, the deity of Shizuhi was closely connected with those shrines and with the {{ill|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11604953|WD=Q11604953|lt=hereditary governors of Kii Province|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}. At Kusa-no-miya, enshrined within the grounds of those shrines, it is said that on September 15 the deity of Shizuhi made a sacred procession and the Shizuhi Festival was held {{Sfn|Kamayama Shrine and Shizuhi Shrine (Kami-gami)|1986|pp=316–318}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; however, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}) it was merged into Inari Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of Kamayama Shrine {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}{{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 25 ({{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|WD=Q18597|1=1950}}), shrine buildings were constructed on the original Mount Yakushi site and the deity was transferred there {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. === Divine ranks === * Records of divine rank promotions in the Six National Histories ** {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 11 ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}) 11th month, 3rd day: from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}*) – recorded as “Shizuhi no Kami.” ** {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}) 10th month, 21st day: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}*) – recorded as “Shizuhi no Kami.” ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) 1st month, 27th day: from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – recorded as “Shizuhi no Kami.” ** Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}) 10th month, 17th day: from Senior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Third Rank (*Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku*) – recorded as “Shizuhi no Kami.” * After the Six National Histories ** Senior First Rank (*{{ill|lt=Kii no Kuni Jinmyōchō|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}*) – recorded as “Shizuhi Ōkami.” === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine historical records * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 31 Place Names of Wakayama Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=458249031X|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Shizuhi Shrine Site"|ref={{Harvid|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Shima Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}. * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.)|author=Maruyama Akinori|year=1986|chapter=Kamayama Shrine and Shizuhi Shrine|title=Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Places – Vol. 6 Ise, Shima, Iga, Kii|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=456002216X|ref={{Harvid|Kamayama Shrine and Shizuhi Shrine (Kami-gami)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Association for Shikinaisha Studies (ed.)|author=Kiyoshi Nakano|chapter=Shizuhi Shrine|year=1987|title=Report on Shikinaisha Surveys, Vol. 23 Nankaidō|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Kamayama Shrine|Q11597743|WD=Q11597743|1=Kamayama Shrine}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shizuhi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shizuhi Shrine|en|Kamayama Shrine|fr|Sanctuaire Kamayama|ja|竈山神社|simple|Kamayama Shrine|WD=Q11597743|1=Kamayama Shrine}} {{commons category|Category:Shizuhi-jinja}} [[JA:静火神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28693031}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Shizuhi Shrine |Image = [[File:Shizuhi-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and shrine buildings |Location = Wada, {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}} |Coordinates= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|12|10.64|N|135|12|28.86|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shizuhi Shrine}} |Deity = Shizuhi Ōkami |Shintai = |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Grand Shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
An extramural subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kamayama Shrine|Q11597743|WD=Q11597743|1=Kamayama Shrine}} |Founded = Unknown
(before {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 11 ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}})) |Main Hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage site = Ki Sansho-sha |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Shizuhi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{Coordinate list}} [[File:Shizuhi-jinja kyuuchi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Former site monument of Shizuhi Shrine (foreground) and Mount Yakushi, where the current shrine stands]] '''Shizuhi Shrine''' (しずひじんじゃ / しづひじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Wada, {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. Today, it is an extramural subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kamayama Shrine|Q11597743|WD=Q11597743|1=Kamayama Shrine}}. Together with {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Shima Shrine|Q17212913|WD=Q17212913|1=Shima Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}, Wakayama City), it is known as the “Ki Sansho-sha” (Three Shrines of Ki) {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following single kami {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}: :* Shizuhi Ōkami According to documents of the Ugai clan, the shrine’s hereditary priests, the deity was identified as {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Musubi no Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. Another theory, found in the *Zoku Fudoki*, holds that the “Ki Sansho-sha” were branch enshrinements of the three deities of Isonokami—Itsu no Take no Mikoto, Ōyatsuhime no Mikoto, and Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto—and identifies the deity of Shizuhi Shrine as Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. Yet another view considers Shizuhi to have been a “fire-quelling deity” of Kii Province, known as the “Land of Trees” {{Sfn|Kamayama Shrine and Shizuhi Shrine (Kami-gami)|1986|pp=316–318}}. In addition, the *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}* states that Funatama Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}, was the clan deity of the Ki clan and the main shrine of “Shima Shrine, Shizuhi Shrine, and Date Shrine.” === History === === Founding === The date of founding is unknown. Originally, the shrine stood about 200 meters east of its current location ({{Coord|34|12|9.46|N|135|12|37.07|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Former site of Shizuhi Shrine}}) {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. According to the *Zoku Fudoki*, Shizuhi Shrine had fallen into ruin before the {{ill|lt=Einin|Q1327307|WD=Q1327307|1=Einin}} era ({{ill|lt=1293|Q5557|WD=Q5557|1=1293}}–{{ill|lt=1299|Q5564|WD=Q5564|1=1299}}), but a document of the Hinokuma Shrine from {{ill|lt=Chōkyō|Q968843|WD=Q968843|1=Chōkyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1487|Q6652|WD=Q6652|1=1487}}) records that one *to* of ritual rice was allocated to Shizuhi Shrine {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. According to the *Nanki Tokugawa-shi*, by around {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1723|Q6921|WD=Q6921|1=1723}}) the shrine had again fallen into ruin; in that same year, Wakayama Domain rebuilt the shrine buildings on Mount Yakushi (Maeyama / Amagiri-yama; the present site), and Kamayama Shrine assumed its administration {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. === Overview === In the national histories, together with Date Shrine and Shima Shrine (Shima Jinja), Shizuhi Shrine is recorded as having received promotions in divine rank: from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 11 ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}); to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}); to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}); and to Junior Third Rank in Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}) {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}*, it is listed under {{ill|lt=Kii Province|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} as “Shizuhi Shrine, Myōjin Grand Shrine,” placing it among the {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}; the *{{ill|lt=Kii no Kuni Jinmyōchō|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}* records it as “Senior First Rank, Shizuhi Ōkami” {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}. In all cases, it is ranked higher than Kamayama Shrine. It is said that together with {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City) and {{ill|lt=Shima Shrine|Q17212913|WD=Q17212913|1=Shima Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}, Wakayama City), it was known as the “Ki Sansho-sha” {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}. References to the Ki Sansho-sha appear beginning with a rice storage ledger from {{ill|lt=Eishō|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549|1=Eishō (Heian period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1048|Q19359|WD=Q19359|1=1048}}), as well as in the *{{ill|lt=Chūyūki|Q11363231|WD=Q11363231|1=Chūyūki}}* entry for {{ill|lt=Tennen|Q1193599|WD=Q1193599|1=Tennin (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1109|Q19515|WD=Q19515|1=1109}}) and in the four-verse sacred songs of the *{{ill|lt=Ryōjin Hishō|Q3010584|WD=Q3010584|1=Ryōjin Hishō}}* {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}. According to the *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}*, the origin of the Ki Sansho-sha lies in the three ships used by {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} for the {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Kingdoms of Korea|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}, which she had {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} enshrine {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=362}}. Furthermore, since Wada Village was formerly a shrine estate of {{ill|lt=Hinokuma Shrine and Kunikakasu Shrine|Q888184|WD=Q888184|1=Hinokuma Shrine}}, the deity of Shizuhi was closely connected with those shrines and with the {{ill|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11604953|WD=Q11604953|lt=hereditary governors of Kii Province|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}. At Kusa-no-miya, enshrined within the grounds of those shrines, it is said that on September 15 the deity of Shizuhi made a sacred procession and the Shizuhi Festival was held {{Sfn|Kamayama Shrine and Shizuhi Shrine (Kami-gami)|1986|pp=316–318}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; however, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}}) it was merged into Inari Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of Kamayama Shrine {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine Site (Heibonsha)|1983|p=376}}{{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 25 ({{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|WD=Q18597|1=1950}}), shrine buildings were constructed on the original Mount Yakushi site and the deity was transferred there {{Sfn|Shizuhi Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987|p=99}}. === Divine ranks === * Records of divine rank promotions in the Six National Histories ** {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 11 ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}) 11th month, 3rd day: from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}*) – recorded as “Shizuhi no Kami.” ** {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}) 10th month, 21st day: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}*) – recorded as “Shizuhi no Kami.” ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) 1st month, 27th day: from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – recorded as “Shizuhi no Kami.” ** Jōgan 17 ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}) 10th month, 17th day: from Senior Fourth Rank,
